methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
LeydenJar Technologies is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Christian Rood and Gabriel de Scheemaker that has raised €23M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 6 same-sector, same-stage private companies, supplemented by reference to 1 public comparable and 8 additional private peers in the Climate & Energy space.

The peer-median method was applied as the primary triangulation approach. The median valuation across the 6-company peer set—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and one additional peer—yielded $355M. The peer cohort exhibits material dispersion: valuations range from $120M at the low end to $5.0B at the high end, reflecting heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within the sector.

This analysis supports a valuation range of $302M to $408M, centered on a median of $355M. The 15% band around the median reflects the observed peer distribution and analytical confidence appropriate to a single-method analysis. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the peer set, path to capital efficiency given the sector's typical burn profiles, and near-term financing runway given the €23M already deployed. A multi-method triangulation incorporating venture capital method or discounted cash flow analysis would narrow this range materially.
